title: Fish and Shellfish
content:
SELECTION

Fresh fish: Elastic flesh firm enough to resist indentation; bright eyes; bright red gills; shiny skin; scales adhering closely; sinks in water. Must be kept on ice; use quickly.

Frozen fish: Cleaned, skinned, boned, filleted, frozen immediately after catching. Must be cooked immediately after thawing.

Shellfish: Oysters, clams, crab, lobster, scallops, shrimp. Available fresh in season or canned.

Salt fish: Mackerel, cod, herring, finnan haddie. Soak overnight before preparing as fresh fish.

PREPARATION

Remove scales, entrails. Fillet or leave whole as desired.

COOKING METHODS

Broil: Baste with butter or oil; cook quickly at high heat.
Bake: In moderate oven with butter, seasonings.
Poach: In 185°F liquid until flesh flakes easily.
Deep-fry: Coat with batter or crumbs; fry at 360-375°F.

SAUCES FOR FISH

Tartare, Hollandaise, tomato, highly seasoned white sauce, mayonnaise, French dressing, cheese, horseradish.

GARNISHES

Crisp parsley, radish roses, lemon slices, orange slices, fresh tomato, cucumber, celery leaves, lettuce hearts, olives, asparagus tips.
